title: HOW STRATHLON WORKS (product help — use this to answer 'how do I…' questions about the app)

sections.name: Getting started
tips: During onboarding, tap 'Import from Apple Health' to pull in weight, height, resting HR, VO2 max and body-fat at once. Strathlon only ever READS from Health, never writes back.

Onboarding includes a private Health & safety step (conditions, medications, pregnancy/breastfeeding) and a short optional fitness-readiness questionnaire. If the user indicates pregnancy or breastfeeding, Strathlon does NOT prescribe a programme for that period: it RECOMMENDS pausing, sets no weight-loss target, and clamps calories to maintenance so a deficit is never shown (it does not stop the app itself - logging stays available). This applies whatever the user's sex is set to; it is driven by the pregnancy and breastfeeding answers, not by sex. These details stay on the device and are never in the default iCloud backup, but they ARE shared with the AI Coach so its advice is safe - see the Privacy section. General info, not medical advice.

AGE & CALORIE SAFETY (build 175). Strathlon's minimum age is 16; 18+ is recommended. Under-16s are blocked at onboarding with a friendly explainer and cannot set up an account. For a 16-17 user, Strathlon NEVER sets a weight-loss/fat-loss target or a calorie deficit: the Fat Loss goal is hidden in setup and calories are clamped to maintenance-or-above, exactly like the pregnancy/breastfeeding safeguard. 16-17 users can still choose Muscle Gain, General Fitness or Sports Performance and use all sport conditioning. If a 16-17 user asks how to lose weight, explain warmly that Strathlon focuses on strength, fitness and performance for under-18s and keeps them fuelled at a healthy energy level - do NOT prescribe a deficit, cut or fast. (Weight-management support for 16-17s with professional guidance is on the roadmap.) SEPARATELY, for EVERY user there is an absolute minimum daily calorie backstop - a daily target is never set below roughly 1200 kcal for women or 1500 kcal for men, whatever the goal. BMR is estimated with the Mifflin-St Jeor equation from sex, weight, height and age (or from lean body mass via the Katch-McArdle equation when the user has enabled body-composition mode) - state this plainly if asked, do not invent a different method.

Set food allergies and supplement ingredients to avoid in onboarding or via More → Profile & Settings → Food preferences — Strathlon treats them as hard, never-suggest exclusions in meal plans and Coach advice.
Replay the guided tour anytime via More → Profile & Settings → 'Replay App Tour'.

sections.name: Training plan & sessions
tips: Today's session is at the top of the Plan tab. The programme periodises through phases that get harder as you progress; tap 'Why this phase?' on a gym day to see the science. The plan opens on the phase you're actually in, and future phases stay locked until you earn them by training (on by default). Tap a locked phase to read what's coming and when it unlocks — your workouts stay on your current phase until then — and a small 'Phase unlocked' note appears when you get there. Anything you've already earned never locks again, even after a fresh cut or a break. Prefer free access to every phase? Turn off 'Lock future phases until earned' in More → Profile & Settings → Training. Maintenance is always available.
Tap an exercise to run rest timers, spoken cues and a 3-2-1 into each set. Tap 'Ask Coach' on any exercise to hand the Coach that exercise's full context for form tips.

Home training days adapt to your equipment and vary week to week. Set what you have at home in More > Profile & Settings > Training (Dumbbells, Resistance Bands, Pull-Up Bar and more). Your home Push/Pull/Legs/Shoulders/Arms/Upper/Full-body days then show only moves you can do — with nothing selected you get a full, complete bodyweight session (push-ups, inverted rows, split squats, glute bridges, pike push-ups and more); add dumbbells or bands and those moves appear. Moves that need a bought item are tagged with it — the Pull-Up needs a Pull-Up Bar, so it is only ever offered if you have ticked one. Moves that use ordinary furniture (inverted rows under a table, chair triceps dips) count as bodyweight and are always available. Every home category always keeps at least six pure-bodyweight options, and the supersets/tri-sets rotate across weeks so home training stays fresh. Advanced moves always sit alongside an easier version. Gym workouts are unchanged. The MAINTENANCE phase is home-aware too (from build 162): with equipment set to Home you get a six-move bodyweight maintenance session on each Push/Pull/Legs/Upper day — five fixed moves plus a sixth that rotates week to week from the same home pool. Push is decline/diamond/pike/archer/wide push-ups; inverted and supinated inverted rows, Superman, prone Y-raise, reverse snow angel; Bulgarian split squat, single-leg Romanian deadlift, single-leg glute bridge, reverse lunge, clamshell; push-up, doorway row, wall pike push-up, bodyweight triceps extension, scapular wall slide. All need only bodyweight and ordinary furniture, and each still adapts to a flagged injury. On a 5-day or 6-day split the Shoulders/Arms/Full Body maintenance days are still gym-oriented — a known gap.
Warm-up, rest and HIIT timers keep running on the Lock Screen and Dynamic Island.

Log working weight × reps on any exercise (pre-filled from last time), or 'Log each set' for detail. Enter kg or lb. Subscribers can tap '📷 Snap to fill' to read the weight off a plate/machine/logbook. Weights auto-fill next time.

Trained away from the app or your phone? Tap 'Mark this session done' on the plan so it still counts (works for earlier days too). Tap again to undo. Auto-captured sessions (guided or Apple Watch) stay locked.
Collapse/expand workout sections (warm-up, exercises, HIIT finisher) by tapping their headers.
Turn on 'Can double up' so double days train the muscles opposite to those your sport fatigues.

When a plan finishes, Strathlon checks you against your goal: close-but-not-there offers a fresh block sized to what's left; goal hit points to Maintenance; stalled tells you honestly and helps regroup.

sections.name: Logging food & the AI Coach
tips: On the AI Coach tab, tap the camera, photograph your food, then Analyse Photo — or just type what you ate ('two eggs on toast and a flat white'). Either returns a macro estimate with one-tap 'Add to Log'.

From Add to Log, pick the day and meal slot — you can back-date meals up to two weeks. Meal slots are Breakfast, Mid-Morning Snack, Lunch, Afternoon Snack, Dinner and Evening Snack (for anything eaten later in the day) — a food is filed under exactly the slot you tap, including anything the AI Coach logs for you. Logged something under the wrong meal? Tap the … next to any logged item in your Tracker food log — today or a past day — to move it to a different meal slot.

The AI Coach knows your goals, sport, today's targets, logged foods and full programme. Ask 'what should I eat before training?', 'build me a meal plan for today' (returns a full day of meals it saves into Nutrition), or 'how's my progress?'.

To turn a saved AI meal plan into a shopping list, open the Nutrition tab, scroll to that day type's meal plan and tap 'Shopping list'. It gathers every ingredient from that day's plan into a tickable checklist grouped by meal (ticks are saved), and the Share button sends a clean plain-text list to Apple Reminders, Notes or your grocery app's search. There is one list per day type (Training, Rest, Match, Sport, Maintenance) and it covers the AI meal plan only — saved favourites aren't included (they carry no ingredient details). If you regenerate that day's plan afterwards, the list shows a 'plan changed' banner with a Refresh that rebuilds it and keeps ticks that still match. It does not add items to a supermarket cart for you — it's a hand-off to get your list into your shopping app.

A free Readiness card at the top of the AI Coach tab gives you a 0-100 readiness score from your sleep, resting heart rate and between-set recovery — the score itself needs no subscription. With no chat open it shows the components it used; once a conversation is running it collapses to a compact line, keeping any caveats. When there isn't enough recent Health data yet it says so instead of guessing. TAPPING the card asks the coach to explain the score: that is a subscriber feature, so a free user is shown the upgrade options instead, and a subscriber who has Health sharing switched off is asked to turn it on first (the coach cannot explain a score it cannot see).

How the readiness score is worked out: it blends three signals - your recent sleep against a 7.5-hour-a-night target, your recent resting heart rate against your OWN baseline rather than a population average, and how far your heart rate drops between sets. Sleep carries about half the weight, resting heart rate about a third, and between-set recovery the rest. The weights stretch to cover whatever data you actually have, so if only sleep and recovery are available the score is built from just those two - and when only ONE signal has enough data the score reflects that signal alone, which the card says in its caveat line. Each signal needs a minimum before it counts - a few nights of sleep, a handful of resting-HR readings, a few rest blocks with heart rate - and a score always needs either sleep or resting heart rate: between-set recovery on its own is not enough, and the card says it hasn't got enough data yet rather than guessing. The exact lookback for each signal is tuned over time, so describe the signals and the weighting rather than quoting a fixed number of days. It is a general guide, not a medical or clinical measure, and it is not comparable with a validated device score. For women, resting-HR and sleep-recovery signals can reflect normal cycle variation.

When you've turned on 'Share Health data with AI coaching' (More → Profile & Settings → App settings → Privacy), the Coach can also see and discuss your Apple Health trends — your sleep in detail (deep/REM/core/awake stages, efficiency and bed/wake times), resting heart rate, heart-rate variability (HRV), respiratory rate, blood oxygen (SpO2), overnight wrist temperature, VO2 max, between-set recovery, weight trend and readiness. Just ask 'how's my sleep been?', 'why's my resting HR up?' or 'am I recovered enough to train hard today?'. It reasons only from those figures, never invents numbers, and it's general guidance, not medical advice. Your cycle data is never shared and stays on its own separate switch.
Strathlon spots foods you log regularly and offers to auto-add them on chosen days; manage rules under Nutrition → Quick Add (hold a food and choose 'Set as daily auto-add').
Nutrition shows evidence-based supplement guidance tuned to your day type, with dose, timing and why.
The AI Coach (chat, meal plans, photo macro scanning, progress analysis) is premium with a 14-day free trial on annual. Plan, timers, targets, supplements, tracker and Health sync are built in.

sections.name: Tracker, weight & progress
tips: The Tracker's top row shows four tiles — Target, Burned, Consumed, Deficit. Target is worked out for your day type and updates live when you add activity. Tap the ⓘ on any tile for its source.

Weight from Apple Health is adopted automatically now — there is no "update your weight?" confirm step. What you SEE on the Tracker is always your latest reading; your calorie and protein targets follow a smoothed ~7-day TREND of your weight, so a single heavy or light day doesn't move them. Type a weight in yourself and that entry wins for that day (for about 48 hours). This uses your Apple Health READ permission, which is separate from sharing Health data with the AI Coach.

The weight chart shows actual weight (solid line) plus predicted weight from your tracked deficit (dashed line, ≈7,700 kcal ≈ 1 kg). They drift apart normally (water, glycogen, adaptation). Premium members can tap 'Ask your coach to explain this gap'. The chart scrolls sideways; rotate or tap ⤢ for full screen with pinch-zoom. Break periods show as a sky-blue band, cycle phases in baby pink. A muted 'Trend' line shows the smoothed ~7-day weight trend that drives your targets.

Open More → Progress Photos to add a photo every week or two (auto-tagged with that day's weight) and tap Compare for before/after. Photos stay on-device by default; optional private-iCloud backup is off by default in More → Profile & Settings → App settings → Privacy & your data.

A 'Your week's ready' card atop the AI Coach tab opens your weekly review (sessions, rest, active burn, energy balance, sleep, weight trend). Tap 'Ask the Coach about this' to chat with your stats pre-loaded. The 'Your AI progress analysis' card (shown with no chat open) gives a quick weekly read plus your coach's take.

The Strength progress card in your weekly review is free (per exercise: heaviest set, estimated 1RM, volume-load, trend, and a deload nudge when a lift stalls). Subscribers can also tap 'See full strength history' — next to that card, and in Your Trends (the Progress button, top-left of the AI Coach tab) — to open a per-exercise chart of how a lift has changed over time: estimated 1RM or top-set (a toggle), personal-record markers, and shaded plateaus, in your chosen weight unit; swipe to scroll your history.

On that strength-history chart, subscribers can tap 'Analyse my strength' to have your AI coach read that lift's progress — it leads with your estimated-1RM trend and calls out any personal record or plateau, using only your logged numbers; if there isn't enough history yet it says so and asks you to log a few more sessions rather than guessing, and you can keep chatting from there. The paid weekly review's written analysis also now includes a short strength paragraph (top movers, any PR, any plateau) when you've logged weighted sets.

sections.name: Apple Watch
tips: One-time setup: open the Strathlon app on the watch and tap Allow for Health. You only do this once per watch.

Start a session on the phone and Strathlon starts the workout on the watch automatically — live heart rate streams in and each block logs to Apple's Workout app with calories. No taps, no subscription. A ⌚ shows when it's live.
Do NOT also start Apple's own Workout app for the same session — that double-counts calories.
Heart rate is colour-coded: amber = below target (push harder), red = at/above target.
Premium adds hands-free control: on the wrist, tap Done to log a set and start rest, tap again during rest to skip it — carries you from warm-up through the HIIT finisher.
Apple's Fitness app lists workouts by duration, not calories; tap a workout to see calories — they ARE recorded.
No watch? Heart rate still comes from Apple Health where available.

sections.name: Activities, sports & goals
tips: Add a walk, run, cycle, swim, gym class, gym or home workout, or any sport to any day (including rest days) via 'Add a session, walk or activity'. IMPORTANT distinction: logging an activity here RAISES today's eating allowance so you can eat a bit more for it — that's the intake side. Recording the session in Apple Health (an Apple Watch workout, or a manual entry) separately raises the calories Apple Health shows you've BURNED — that's the other side of the same ledger. The two are meant to OFFSET each other, not add together: log the activity here for the eating allowance, and record it in Apple Health too so it also counts toward your burn. Add distance (km) to runs/walks for a sharper estimate; enter your own burn from a watch if you have it.
For gym classes, open Gym classes and search the name (e.g. 'rpm', 'bodypump'); calories scale to your weight and you can tweak duration or pin your own number.

For a gym or home workout, open the 'Gym & home' tile: pick Push/Pull/Leg/Upper day, a full-body/circuit, home bodyweight or home dumbbell — or 'Other workout (custom)'. Search by the name you use ('ppl', 'leg day', 'calisthenics'); calories scale to your weight and duration, or turn on Override to enter your own from a watch. It's for EXTRA or unplanned work — your planned gym session is already counted, so it's never double-counted.
Set each sport's Intensity (Light/Standard/High) in More → Profile & Settings → Sports to tune its calorie bonus. With multiple sports, the primary gets the full bonus and others scale.
When a programme completes, switch to Maintenance: calories rise to full TDEE with carbs raised gradually over a 4-week reverse diet.
Going away? Plan tab → 'On a break or catching up?' pauses your plan (calories ease to maintenance, no deficit, no 'missed day' nagging) and lets you tick days you trained while away.
Change units (kg/lb/stone) in More → Profile & Settings → Body & Stats → Units; everything reflows and saved data is unchanged.

sections.name: Privacy
tips: The 'Share Health data with AI coaching' switch (More -> Profile & Settings -> App settings -> Privacy & your data) covers APPLE HEALTH MEASUREMENTS ONLY: weight, BMI and body composition, metabolic rate (BMR), calories burned and recent deficit, heart rate, heart-rate variability (HRV) and between-set recovery, respiratory rate, blood oxygen (SpO2), overnight wrist temperature, cardio fitness (VO2 max), recent off-app workouts, your sleep in detail (total sleep and sleep debt, the deep/REM/core/awake breakdown, sleep efficiency, and your bed and wake times), weight history, and the derived readiness score. It is off by default; turning it off keeps all of those on the phone. It does NOT control the health & safety details the user typed in - see the next tip.

The health & safety details the user TYPED IN THEMSELVES are always shared with the AI Coach on every message and are NOT controlled by the 'Share Health data' switch: allergies and supplements to avoid, pregnancy and breastfeeding, chronic conditions, medications, and injuries. This is deliberate - a coach that does not know about an allergy, a pregnancy or a heart medication could give unsafe advice. If a user asks what the coach knows about them, say this plainly; never tell them these details are withheld.

Food preferences are also always shared with the coach, including dietary or religious preferences such as halal, kosher, vegetarian or vegan, plus preferred cuisines and cooking time, so meal ideas suit the user.
Users can see the full list any time: More -> Profile & Settings -> App settings -> Privacy & your data -> 'What your AI coach can see'.

What happens to the data sent to the AI: it is never sold, never used for advertising, and never used to train AI models. To keep follow-up questions fast, the AI provider briefly caches the BACKGROUND CONTEXT we send (a few minutes); beyond that our processors apply only their own limited operational retention, such as short-term abuse prevention. Strathlon keeps no long-term store of messages. Do NOT tell a user their messages are simply 'discarded' - that overstates what we know.
Cycle-aware coaching is a separate, off-by-default switch; if on, the coach sees only a general phase label (e.g. 'pre-menstrual'), never dates, flow, symptoms or history.

The tracker mirrors food logs, saved foods and auto-add rules to your private iCloud (no account, raw meals never uploaded to Strathlon); turn off in More -> Profile & Settings -> App settings -> Privacy & your data.

The default-ON 'iCloud backup' switch includes basic details, weight, daily totals, goals, training setup, plan progress AND food preferences - including any dietary or religious preference set, such as halal or kosher. It does NOT include health & safety details, and it does NOT include allergies; those have their own separate, off-by-default switch ('Back up health & safety details').
Suggest and vote on features via More -> 'App roadmap & Feedback'.

sections.name: Onboarding & your profile in detail
tips: There is no account, sign-up or password — your profile, logs and full history live on your device, so nothing to remember and nothing to lose to a forgotten password.

First-run setup builds the plan around you: your body details, your goal, your sport (pick from any category across 38 sports), and your weekly training/match schedule. You can edit these later in More → Profile & Settings (to change your goal and experience, use More → Profile & Settings → Reset & Data → 'Restart and redo onboarding', which keeps all your logged history).

Manage your data in More → Profile & Settings → Reset & Data. RESTART: 'Restart my plan' puts your plan back to Week 1 today (keeps all history), and 'Restart and redo onboarding' lets you change your goal or experience (also keeps history). CLEAR SPECIFIC DATA: tick just what you want gone — Nutrition log, Weight & body, Lifting history, Holiday periods, Coach conversations or Cycle data — everything is off by default, and what you clear is removed both on this device and from your private iCloud. DELETE EVERYTHING: type DELETE to erase all your data and your iCloud backup and start fresh — that now includes your saved foods, auto-add rules, saved meal plans, schedule changes, profile photo and any stored voice key, and nothing is copied back afterwards. Clearing Cycle data asks a second time and never touches Apple Health. It cannot be undone and Strathlon keeps no hidden recoverable copy, so use 'Export my data first' (a CSV + JSON copy) at the top of that screen before you clear or delete.

The readiness check is a short, optional set of yes/no questions — the kind a trainer asks before you start. If an answer stands out, Strathlon gently suggests getting medical clearance first. It is a readiness check, not medical advice. The individual answers stay on-device; the coach is told only that a readiness item was flagged (and whether clearance was confirmed), so it can keep guidance conservative.

The health & safety step captures pregnancy/breastfeeding, ongoing conditions, medications, allergies, and any food or supplement ingredients to avoid. Meal plans and the Coach treat allergies and avoid-ingredients as HARD, never-suggest exclusions.
You are asked ONCE whether the AI Coach may see your Apple Health figures. It is off by default and can be changed any time in More → Profile & Settings → App settings → Privacy & your data.

To edit anything later: More → Profile & Settings. Body & Stats holds your measurements and units (your goal is read-only there — change it via Reset & Data → 'Restart and redo onboarding'); the Health-data sharing switch is under App settings → Privacy & your data, and cycle-phase sharing under HEALTH → Cycle-aware insights.

Resting heart rate and VO2 max are optional in setup and must be realistic to be accepted: resting HR between 30 and 150 bpm, VO2 max between 10 and 100 ml/kg/min. If you enter something outside that it is not saved -- and if you already had a value saved, that one is kept -- and the field tells you which happened. You can change both later without redoing setup: More -> Profile & Settings -> Body & stats has a Resting HR and a VO2 max stepper. Your five heart-rate zones appear under More -> Profile & Settings -> Your targets, worked out from your age-based maximum HR and your resting HR. If a value saved by an older version is outside the plausible range, the zones are worked out from a default of 60 bpm and a note above them says so. If the gap between your resting HR and your maximum is too small to give meaningful zones, the ladder is replaced by a note explaining that. Either way, adjust your resting HR in Body & stats to fix it.

sections.name: Running a workout, timers & supersets
tips: The workout screen is split into fold-out sections — warm-up, your main workout, and a HIIT finisher. Warm-up and HIIT start folded to cut scrolling; your main workout stays open. Tap any header to collapse or expand it.

Warm-up, rest and HIIT countdowns keep running on the Lock Screen and in the Dynamic Island with spoken cues, so you can put the phone down between sets. The voice coach calls each exercise and set including the rep target, with a 3-2-1 into each work set.

Some training days end with a HIIT finisher and a short core finisher (such as plank, dead bug or glute bridge march). It tops up to 2 core moves for the day in total, counting any core work your sport accessories already include — so it may add 0, 1 or 2 moves — and it never repeats the same movement pattern (e.g. rotational) twice. Core is added on strength days only — not on rest, sport-only, maintenance or gym-plus-sport double days — and it rotates week to week.

Both the HIIT finisher and the core finisher adapt to any injury you flag. HIIT filters its movements for all injury types (a shoulder injury hides overhead and forceful-arm moves; a lower-back injury hides jumps, hinges and crunches; an elbow injury hides gripping moves; knee/ankle keep the low-impact set), always leaving at least six safe options, and shows an 'Adapted for…' banner. The core finisher likewise only ever shows core work that is safe for your injuries.
Log working weight × reps on any exercise (pre-filled from last time), or choose 'Log each set' for per-set detail. Enter kg or lb. Bodyweight moves can be saved with a weight of 0.
Subscribers can tap 'Snap to fill' to photograph a dumbbell, plate or machine display and Strathlon reads the number off it. Weights then auto-fill next time, so you just confirm or nudge them.

Where the plan pairs exercises into groups, each group header has its own switch. Leave it on to run that group back-to-back as a superset/tri-set; turn it off to do those exercises one at a time as straight sets, each with its own start button and rest between every set. The app remembers the choice per pairing, so a combination that never works at your gym stays as straight sets. On a switched-off group, tap the info button on its header for how to keep the same training density.

Trained away from the app or your phone? Tap 'Mark this session done' so it still counts (works for earlier days too); tap again to undo. Sessions captured automatically (a guided session or an Apple Watch workout) stay locked so real training is never removed by accident.

sections.name: The weight chart & energy-balance in detail
tips: The weight chart plots your ACTUAL weight as a solid line and your PREDICTED weight (from your tracked calorie deficit, at roughly 7,700 kcal per kg) as a dashed line. A third muted 'Trend' line is the smoothed ~7-day weight trend Strathlon uses to set your calorie and protein targets. Reading them together shows whether real change is tracking the maths.
The two lines drift apart normally — real weight often drops faster at first from water and glycogen, then the prediction can run ahead as your body adapts. That divergence is expected, not an error.
Tap the expand button or rotate to landscape for a full-screen chart with pinch-zoom and a drag crosshair. The chart scrolls sideways through your history.
Break periods show as a sky-blue band and, if cycle-aware coaching is on, cycle phases show in baby pink — so context around a plateau is visible at a glance.
Premium members can tap 'Ask your coach to explain this gap' to open a chat with the numbers pre-loaded; the coach usually asks a question or two before explaining rather than guessing.

The Tracker's four top tiles are Target, Burned, Consumed and Deficit. Target is computed for your day type and updates live as you add activity. Tap the info icon on any tile to see exactly where its number comes from.

sections.name: History, export & data ownership
tips: Open Tracker → History for your daily log, newest first — calories in, calories out and weight for each day.

Missed a day? Tracker → History → 'Add missed day', pick the date, then fill in calories consumed and burned (burned is pulled from Apple Health where it has data) and optionally a weight. The calendar covers yesterday and earlier — today is edited on the Tracker screen itself, where the burn is live from Apple Health (whichever device or app writes it there — an Apple Watch if you wear one, otherwise your iPhone's motion sensors). When Apple Health has no burn data for today at all, the Burned and Deficit tiles show a dash rather than a zero, so no deficit or surplus is implied.

Picking a date you have ALREADY logged is safe: the sheet switches to 'Edit Day', shows what that day currently holds, and arrives with your saved values already filled in. Saving updates only the boxes shown — your logged food items and your protein/carbs/fat are always kept. Leaving a box empty means 'leave that value as it is', not zero.
To correct just a weigh-in for a past day, open 'Add missed day' for that date and change only the weight — the calorie boxes can be left empty.
Use Import / Export in History for a CSV round-trip of your daily log, so your data is portable and yours to keep.

Because there is no account, everything lives on-device by default. In More → Profile & Settings → App settings → Privacy & your data you control each backup separately: 'iCloud backup' (on by default) covers your basic details, weight, daily totals, goals, training setup, plan progress and food preferences - including any dietary or religious preference such as halal, kosher, vegetarian or vegan; 'Back up training data to iCloud' (ON by default) covers your weigh-ins and lifting history; 'Back up health & safety details' and 'Sync progress photos to iCloud' are each OFF by default and cover your allergies/injuries/conditions and your progress photos respectively. All backups go to your own private iCloud only, never a Strathlon server. Cycle data is never included in any of them.
Raw meals and photos are never uploaded to Strathlon's servers. Health data is only ever read, never written back to Apple Health.
Change units (kg / lb / stone) any time in More → Profile & Settings → Body & Stats → Units; the whole app reflows and your saved data is unchanged underneath.

If you reinstall or move to a new phone and restore your backup, your health & safety details will NOT come back unless you had turned 'Back up health & safety details' on — it is off by default. Strathlon warns you before you restore and keeps a reminder at the top of your Profile until you re-enter them, because until you do, your meals and plan cannot take your allergies, injuries, conditions or medications into account.

sections.name: Notifications, nuggets & learning
tips: A daily knowledge nugget card surfaces a short, evidence-based tip; some nuggets carry a 'Learn more' link that opens a fuller article on strathlon.com.

Two local notifications are ON by default, controlled in More → Profile & Settings → App settings → Notifications & Tips: 'Daily tip notification' (one science-backed tip each morning, around 9am) and 'Bedtime reminder' (a gentle nightly wind-down reminder, with extra recovery emphasis after a hard training day, around 10:45pm). Notification permission is asked once, near the end of onboarding — if declined, both stay switched on in Settings but won't actually notify until permission is granted (Settings app → Notifications → Strathlon), and Strathlon never re-prompts automatically.

The weekly review card ('Your week's ready') sits atop the AI Coach tab and opens a summary of sessions, rest, active burn, energy balance, sleep and weight trend. Tap 'Ask the Coach about this' to chat with those stats pre-loaded.
The 'Your AI progress analysis' card (shown when no chat is open) gives a quick weekly read plus your coach's take, without you having to ask.

Deeper guides and evidence articles live on the website — the Learn library, per-stat explainer pages, and topic guides (building muscle, fat loss, training with your cycle, supplements, recovery, habits). Ask the Coach and it can point you to the relevant one.
